reviews.review_text:
Run for 10 minutes to die by a bullet that you will never see, wait 30 secs to respawn, repeat.
10/10
i was the gunner of a tank crew and fought 3 or 4 german tanks off after my entire crew died. i felt like john bernthal in fury. 10/10

As a new player, this game feels much more fleshed out than any CoD or Battlefield match I’ve ever played. Classes have actual impact on the outcome of matches.

For fellow new players, take your time and go slow, also follow your squad leader you’ll be aight.

100/10
More like a pixel shooting game. Camping in bushes, waiting for someone to move, dot/pixel shootin. Getting lazer beamed across map while dealing with stuttering. Peace of ♥♥♥♥.

I couldnt even enter a game even with lowest settings and empty servers, waited for 20 minute but it loads and loads, this game was working fine before.

Something weird's going on.
It's a game you really can't play without a mic. Once you learn how to play it's reward but challenging at the beginning. Well worth trying.
this is the most annoying but fun game i have ever played, i saw this guy get blown into a milion pieces would recommend to all ages
although in my opinion its not my type of game, i still enjoyed it alot! i perfer bf1 type gameplay but i still recommend it for people that want hyper realistic ww2!
love spending an hour just sitting in the menu trying to join a game just for there to be empty servers or maxed queue lobbies.
Game is super immersive. Utilizing your role properly to help out your squad feels rewarding. The community is 10/10 and very beginner friendly. You get to commit war crimes. It's a blast.
This game was fun back when the old devs had it now that they decided to sell out and move away so to speak Team 17 has turned it into call of duty light. Not the gem it used to be. #disgusting

One of the worst shooters ever. 
You are unable to see anything at a distance thanks to only terrible graphics settings.
The servers are piles of the most toxic, obnoxious, elitist and sweatiest players ever.
You constantly get spawncamped because the maps are too small.

It baffles me that this game is somehow popular and liked.

Awful visual clarity. I've played thousands of hours in Arma and DayZ and it's not even on the same level.

Mike Erhmantraut said no half-measures, and this game feels like a half-measure between realism and arcade, and it just doesn't blend well imo.

That being said the Tank Mechanics are good, that's about it though.
i played this game way too much but its just that good! The most fun you can have is chatting with squad mates while getting pinned down. Best game

I used to play this a ton years ago. I come back here and there to see how its going. The game has changed a ton over the past few years. The game feels different since the new devs took over. Its not a horrible game, but the game is just full of sweats who play it consistently for the past few years. The game is still very immersive, but its not what I remember it was with the old devs. For that I put this as a negative. Still give it a try as a new player, but be ready to die over and over again until you git gud.

This game would be awesome if it weren't for the RAMPANT hackers that infect every single server. You think CoD and BF are bad? Try playing a game with no kill cam, no way to report with evidence, and no minimap to even know where the shot came from. The whole point of the game is for it to feel more realistic by removing minimaps, hit markers, and shot reticles. Everyone has ruined that with aim hacks.

I wanted to like this game, I really did. Had it on my wishlist for a long time and when a sale came up, I grabbed it. Having given in an honest try.....I'm requesting a refund. The game is unplayable for anyone who doesnt want to download an aimbot. Shame.
I couldnt even join a game it kicked me out and took 10 minutes to load into the match! I am really getting sick of these unreal engine 5 trash

Hell Let Loose is a pretty good semi-authentic WW2 shooter. It sits right in between Battlefield and Squad on mostly all it's mechanics. Performance isn't bad, the gunplay and movement are enjoyable, and if you choose the right servers you get people that are willing to communicate and have fun. I hope it's supported by the devs for at least the next 5-7 years with them releasing Hell let Loose: Vietnam🤞

This game is full of larping war veterans. 99% of servers have level requirements to play certain classes so if you don't have a nerd on your team you're running for 5 minutes (Literally) just to get 1 shot by some dude sitting in a random spot who forgot he has fingers for movement. If you are a masochist you'll love this game.

"Ahhh have not played this in a while. Why not?"
Load into game
Join queue
Wait 15-20 mins
Can't join game
Do this 10x
Decide to join a game half way across America (200 ping)
Die 20 times per game with maybe a kill
BOUGHT OUT AND RUINED. Game lost it's soul and now it's infested with no mic CoD players. Glad to have been here from the start so I at least experienced the best it had to offer.
Everyone is either toxic or Chinese. Sometimes both.
slower paced war every match is different after 100 hours its a great game i met a lot of great people  9/10
Great game, one of the best WW2 shooters out, cannot wait for Vietnam
Highly engaging gameplay and strategic communications focus makes this a must for anyone into the MilSim or realism based gaming genre.

Its got a steep learning curve if your new to shooter games, but otherwise if you have good aim and coordination and work with your team its pretty fun. What I believe gives it a steep learning curve is the maps. If you don't know all the maps by the back of your hand its pretty hard. I only have 42 hours and the main reason I feels harder is because of the sheer amount of maps.
Too slow. Too boring. If you like walking, crawling and hiding around for 95% of the time and fighting for 5% this game is for you. If you lack patience or want action avoid HLL like the plague.

This is NOT Call of Duty! and that is honestly what i like about it. its far more of a MilSim where communication, team work, and map control is what it takes to obtain victory. its got a strong learning curve and you will likely spend a lot of time dying and not even knowing what hit you. but hang in there it gets a little better. my favorite role is Artillery. its so satisfying when the command chat lights up as you wipe a garry and a half a team with a well placed arty shell.

Mostly a fun community and gameplay loop
Don't download if you want all achievements though, the developers were on heavy crack while making them

Literally unplayable, every time I try to load in these days I lose connection to the host the second I load into the server.

Used to be I could get in and play games just fine, then after all the updates and the changes in ... ownership? Whatever it was that went down, it screwed everything up and now I literally can't play at all. I've tried everything, fresh install, verifying game files, deleting the EAC and EAC Launcher and re-verifying to get new files, friggin' everything. Nothing works.

Who do I complain to? What recourse do I have? Please help.
A breath of freshair, treat yourself. In this day and age of gaming you dont get game doing it right anymore... Hell let loose is the exception and this is a masterpeice. Cant wait for HLL Vietnam.
The best shooter you can get online. Servers always getting full in time. What are you waiting for? Get the game, im waiting for you in the unit as your officer <3
Immersive, fun, relies on communication and teamwork. This game has drawn me back into gaming in a time when i couldn't decide what to play for hours. Lots of fun if you can work well together.
Great game. learning curve is a bit steep, but well worth sticking it out - very rewarding.

Você passa 20 minutos correndo pela lama, passando de trincheira em trincheira e logo antes de chegar no ponto de captura você morre com um tiro que saiu do c* do Judas. 100% frustração! isso sim é simulador de guerra. RECOMENDO!
Great game. Very easy to learn. Good introductory game if you're starting to get into the mil-sim genere. Very difficult at first, but once it clicks its hard to let go.

Best FPS game ive ever played, and as a big fan of WW2 this is perfection for me. 

Only downside is the messed up moderation system on majority of servers, if you get banned on one server your banned on 90% of servers. Remove barricade ban!
It's fun to play but optimisation is terrible. If you turn your crosshair fast you get fps drops. I usually get 160 fps but it goes down to 70's.

great game but can be annoying if you aren't used to games where you have to look for people anywhere. You can be alone, at least you think, run and just get shot from a mile away from a guy proning and you have no idea where. Once you slow your gameplay down the game is very fun. I do have quite a few issues with vaulting in this game. Sometimes you try to vault a ledge that a foot tall and you character just jumps at it over and over again. On some bush lines where there is a small hill there will be a massive invisible barrier for some reason and you can't even get 6 ft from it despite the area being flat and open. I also have had an issue for at least 3 years of playing where if you prone on a tree you get stuck and have to redeploy otherwise your just stuck to the tree the entire game. Foliage is kinda hard to see through because it can become sort of pixelated at times despite me being at 1440p and epic foliage and most other settings or at least high on other settings. But those aren't horrible. My biggest issue with this game is the inability to change you fps unless you go through the files and find the setting in one of the files and change it there and even then sometimes it doesn't work. There should be an fps cap setting, I don't get why there wasn't that from the beginning, seems like sort of a given.

Now for things I want to see added is I want to see the front in the pacific. America v. Japan, China v. Japan, or any others that were caught in the war by Japan. Squad 44 seems to be the only game to have it and they did a very good job at making it, and the tunnels are phenomenal but the game has a lack of a player base so naturally Hell let loose will have to do even though I'd love to be playing the Pacific front.
The only game where you can use the trench gun to turn nazis into salsa.
games got po0tential, but its been out too long now for issues to be addressed.

This game is a good middle ground if you're looking for a hardcore and/or WW2 shooter that isn't too punishing and still retains most arcade shooter mechanics, like unlimited respawns, plentiful ammo, and live minimap. The community is mostly having fun in the game, not very heavy with larpers. Also, this game, aside from separate voice channels for teamwide and squad, has proximity voice chat, so you will be hearing your teammates speak a lot. You do need to cover down the basic knowledge in order to play the game with a clearer goal than just killing enemies on sight, and it's a very steep process, so you'll either enjoy this like a masochist or you'll quit in the first 5 hours.

One thing to note is that Unreal Engine is one hell of an unstable engine to run. I'm speaking on behalf of this in a budget gaming laptop that barely shaves the recommended specs. It WILL crash often and you'll have to sacrifice looks for the sake of performance (and less crashes). Servers are plenty but of course being an Asian myself, the game has virtually no traction here so you have to migrate to, well, every other region.
The ultimate WW2 1st person game, very funny to play with friend but you must have a nice pc to play it cuz if you don't play with maximum graphic  you're cooked...
just a fun milsim with intense battles. have over 500 hours on playstation, highly recomend using your mic when playing
This game will teach you everything you need to know about being good with people on a team. It's also fun sometimes, thank you I yield the rest of my time.

its the best ww2 game out and its a blast when people are working as a team. If you get this game have a mic and be ready to communicate and learn from other players. If you're going to play it like call of duty and run around mindlessly don't get this game, there's enough of people like you playing already.
Very fun to competitive and semi-hardcore gamers out there and those interested in a fun ww2 multiplayer game

it is the best WW2 millsim game to date in my opinion and i love it. It is fun and i feel it focuses on realism the right amount while still leaving room for some arcady fun and the "gun-paly" is grate, the models and animations are super good 

i also find that for my hardware it is a very well optimized game and it runs well

Top tier game.
Get some good comms and let loose.
Win the day with your communication and the heroes on the battle field.
Stick together! Don't be a blueberry!

some of the most fun times but i cant ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ see anything is that a pixel? idk maybe its a person i shoot at it and shoot all ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ day and check the stats at end and i killed no one lol so wtf was i doing all day xD
fun for a very specific audience. if thats you, youll love it, if not youll hate it. simple
Amazing game where you need to rely on teamwork and communication. Hope that some day they will introduce Japan teatre of war.

Get into a game after waiting in a queue for about 5-6 minutes. 
See an armor squad has 2/3 people so I join and let them both know I’ve done the tutorials but this is my first game. 
Get placed in the gunner spot. 
Proceed to absolutely wipe every tank and infantry we set our eyes on for 4-5 games in a row and only die when out of ammo or run into a tank too big for us.

Absolute peak gameplay, team and server.
very fun game but with big learning curve but still enjoyable

It's a fine tactical shooter, difficult but satisfying once you understand it. The only issue I have with it is that there is no matchmaking. It's great to have a server browser but there's lots of servers at full capacity who also have full waiting queues. Those queues could easily fill an entire new server if the players had an option to matchmake instead of wait to play on a full server
the first time i reinstalled the game it never worked again it just says lost connection to server host
Every time this ptsd simulator causes me to wake screaming in the night, I know its done its job.

If you play at a resolution higher than 1080P it is a huge pain to get the game to run in the right resolution. This is a known issue that has been around for years apparently and the devs just don't care. I refunded this because what other issues will I find with a game if the devs can't be bothered to fix a basic gamebreaking issue?
This is the best coop game on the market, good most the time, but every now and then you just manage to find the right squad, then its fantastic fun.

It took me 9 hours to hear "if the Nazi's won the world would be a better place."

For real, the game looks decent, runs okay, has a fine layout with good maps. The issue? There is little teamwork and communication. It is mostly a bunch of people running around and doing their own thing. Some of the other titles in this category tried for better or worse to incentivize playing as a squad and squads coordinating together to play as a team. With the size of the maps and battles it doesn't quite work as an arcade run and do your own thing type of shooter but there is nothing forcing us to work together.

I will try to keep playing for a bit to see if there are servers that have teamwork and if there are I may change the review. I can't recommend it right now as there are better arcade and better simulation type games out there. If you really want WW2 then this is kind of it right now and that is slightly disappointing.

Great game! I love spending my time with the (usually) fun community in these massive battles. Hope Vietnam lives up to the hype.

The maps are great too! Sure hope to play them sometime but it seems were loading into Sainte-Mère...again.

I swear its the "DUST 2/Mirage" of Hell Let Loose...
remember playing this game all the time several years ago. Now I can't even join a server due to connection issues

This game is really fun if you have the right specs. I really like the details and how the game plays and it can be really fun. But if your computer is not that great I wouldn't really recommend it, maybe get it on xbox instead.
Fantastic game!!! Wish they brought out the Pacific Theatre of War into it. Fighting on Iwo Jima, or Pelilu would be hectic.

I'm already addicted, {Help ME}, reminds me of Heroes & Generals, and I was quite good at that game, a tad more difficult to run this game, but it looks, and feels much better than Heroes & Generals, plus no MTX which was my most hated thing with Heroes & Generals. 

This is very much an immersion-based WW2 Game. No handholding, no third-person, and all death no mercy. Worth every penny, and I got this on sale!

I would of been happy paying full price.

Game can be one of the most fun and holistically engaging FPS on the market when played correctly. If you just use a mic and actually coordinate as team elements, squads to squads, and commander provides supporting efforts, its awesome. No mic games make it way less fun but you can still just mess around if your team is terrible and theyll all leave and hopefully the next match you get people using comms

NAH. Im sure this is a good game but as someone who doesnt want to have suicidal ideation im gunna uninstall and most likely never touch this again. Getting killed from god only knows where is not fun to me.
Get this game right now. Just download it and play. Then watch some videos on Youtube to get better and then play some more.
You get full emersion when you use the comms systems. A headset and microphone are required for best experience. This game isn't for shy people who don't communicate.
Steep learning curve, but once you meet some cool people, the game gets really fun. I know it's pretty hard at the start, but its extremely rewarding, so push through twin.
wonderful game, toxic community with people who think they're better than everyone else for playing a WW2 game for more hours. take a deep breath and let people have fun

This game is unlike any other shooter I've played. The teamwork is second to none, there are matches that friends of mine talk about years later. 

That said, it has a very steep learning curve and it's always going to be a roll of the dice with the kind of people you'll be playing with. 9 times out of 10 though, the people you meet are great.

If you want an action-packed mil-sim, check this game out.

Really give's you the most nearing experience of how WW2 must have felt for those brave Heroes who served during ww2,10/10 game.Gives you the feeling they must have felt in the trenches and in the town's they fought in.

Best and most realistic FPS out there. Play any role you want but teamplay & comms ultimately important to get a good experience out of this game. If you are a solo player thwn don't get this game, its not for you and you will spoil the game for us as well. If you are a team player and you like to make new friends, then this is a MUST BUY.
Wonderful game. I get lost in the combat and tactics. Feels immersive and I get emotionally attached to the matches.
When its good, its really good. When its bad, its like being stuck on a squad with every coworker you have ever hated from every job you've worked at.

This game is not for everyone, but I would still recommend it. It requires a microphone and teamwork. Many players do not mind helping you figure out the fundamentals, however, there are quite a bit of fundamentals and you will die a lot.
First few hours were quite impressive. Very immersive game, super realistic... will require some time and effort (not your run and gun Call of Duty).

I love this game, it is unique no other games comes close at least in my experience, to play a match were the teams are evenly skilled is an exiting experience that no other shooter or even team game that I have played comes close to, the game runs well on my pc which for today standards is potato specs.
Absolute Trash. Run for 30 minutes while idiots burp into voice chat. No one tells you where to go. You just run run run then die die die. I'm sure some Russian hackers are enjoying it though...
Ever since Hell Let Loose updated their Anti Cheat, you cant play this game on Shadow PC due to conflicts with virtual machine. And apparently HLL developers do not care, in my opinion.

Absolutely love the realism of getting shot and dying pretty much from one hit maybe two. It is a team game. If people try and play solo it isnt going to work. The Officers have to coordinate with the commander if there is any hope of the team winning. I love this game fir that.
Amazing, immersive, strategic and on top of all lots of fun if you have the patience and will to cooperate with your team.
a slower game, dont play like cod of bf and you will enjoy. Give it 6 hours to really lock in the game play mechanics. communication is key

Game is good, community is bad, anti cheat is horrible. The anti cheat will block you from playing cause your PC has a cat picture hidden somewhere in it's files, while at the same time the anti cheat will let obvious cheaters in like they're family. Did I mention the community is bad? Honestly it's a coinflip, either nicest unc you've ever met teaches you the game or commander discord mod executes you for desertion

1. Gunplay sucks, recoil is so unbearable its amazing anyone can tolerate trying to shoot someone with one bullet at point blank without getting motion sickness. 
2. Movement sucks, its just nothing but crouch walking because the devs were too lazy to make more than two maps that had more than two buildings, its just crawling up and down slopes simulator
3. Servers suck, its all community servers which have either less than 10 people, or 99 people with a queue you have to wait 15-20 minutes in. 
4. Visibility Sucks. Best of all, the biggest sin of all, despite all of the maps being wide open fields, there is literally zero way to indicate you are looking at a person unless if they are running around in the open. 

You want to play an actually fun "realistic" shooter? Go buy Rising Storm 2: Vietnam, don't get your hopes up for Hell Let Loose Vietnam, it'll likely suck.

Great game but a tough learning curve. For your first 20 hours you will continuously die without seeing the enemy. When you learn to take things slowly and look for movement things improve.
If you have a great squad the experience is amazing, with enemy call outs, tactics, teamwork, direction etc etc. That's how the game is meant to be played.
I'd definitely recommend the game 😀

This is probably going to be one of the best FPS cinematic looking shooters out there.
Visually unbelievable jeff
The Combat is unreal and realistic as.
The pings you get off a head shot are sexual but when done to you its heart breaking.
This game is run off communication and coordination with the commander. If you fail to do so  then bugger off to BF or COD, you will not be missed.
If you are happy to take orders and understand the concept of the tactics involved you will thrive in this game.
This game is unbelievable when it come to realistic warfare and FFS  there is no game like it and should be treated with that respect.
I you like the fact you may die from 1 shot but also may kill by 1 shot then this is the game for you. It is command based and to win the battle you must support your commander and other squads combined.
Genuinely if you have the patience to play this it is totally worth it.
Anyone that disagrees fancies their mother.

HLL average experiance: spend an hour and a half prone in a bush having no idea where im being shot from then u get hit by some random ahh artillery strike respawn get shot instantly then the medic runs right past u then your pc blows up because hll is a grafic demanding game then u rage quit.  10/10 my favourite game on steam

Game is brutally fun.  The realism and team work are fantastic.  Can be frustrating if you are used to BF or COD style game play.  When you get a good commander, and all the squad and teams are working together and playing their roles well, the game feels much more immersive

A polished, immersive WW2 fps that looks and sounds great. I like the military sim level of detail in terms of unique roles to play (sniper, engineer, tank commander, rifleman etc); the authentic maps, weapons and forces; the interconnectedness of building supply chains and squads working in unison; the satisfying levelling up; and the emergent gameplay of 50vs 50 PvP. The gameplay revolves around tactical placement (and destruction) of spawn points rather than kills, and the game has a refreshing pace and tempo compared to the sugar rush of arcade shooters like Cod and Battlefield. 

What I don't like is the variable gameplay experience which is highly dependent on the quality of the player base in the server, with a lack of communication really hampering your ability to play the game effectively. Adding to the potential misery of a game session is the long wait times to get into servers and the brutal skill gap (with always a hint of suspicion) which sees you getting shot regularly out of nowhere. A slightly dysfunctional team means that in Warfare mode (by far the most popular mode) you will get steamrolled with very little agency to turn the tide of the game. 

On PC, I've needed to look up numerous Reddit posts to ensure it's optimized but once I've set all the dials right, I've noticed very little performance issues. The servers are listed with their ping ratings, so you can generally avoid laggy games. 

Overall, HLL is a keeper and the risk reward of finding a good game/server session is worth the pain of playing a running simulator where you get shot and have to rinse and repeat. Finding a squad working together and communicating effectively can make for a top tier gaming experience.

I want to give this a positive review but I just cannot.

Suppression hits like a freight train after taking 2-3 near misses.
MG's are laser beams.
Bolt-Actions are slow to rechamber.
SMG's only work under 10m.
Lack of decent anti-cheat which is clear from being headshot from 200m when you are trying to see anything to shoot.
Lack of actual run speed... like seriously, crouch walking speed is the same as standing walking speed. Same goes for sprint speed, which is unlimited but has no actual speed to it. Top speed 1m/s when the average person can do 5m/s. It's the same for all soldier classes too, making any move from cover to cover impossible to do without getting pegged like the easiest setting at a fun fair bb gun game.
Quickest death in the last hour I played - 14seconds because of massive open fields and moved to try and get through a gap in the hedge row to receive a magical single rifle round to my dome... whilst moving, whilst not even able to be seen prior...


Get this game if you like constantly waiting to respawn back in with a bleed out system (where only one person per squad can revive)... creating a 5 second delay to hold F to bleed out... followed by a further 5 second delay waiting for the option to press space to respawn... followed by up to a 40 second wave delay based on what type of spawn you chose to come back on.

You'll honestly spend more time going through the respawn cycle than you actually will playing the game... which turns into a running (I use this term figuratively, we all know the soldiers in this game have the cardio endurance of an asthmatic sloth) simulator.

I would wait untill its fixed.
you cant even get into a server even after doing all the fixes.
The game is just broken
shuts down after 40 min
never loads into a game.
let alone takes 5 years just to get through the ANTI cheat
A truly fantastic game, made better by its community.

The 20 first hours of this game was like hell week.. I almost broke my pc for how many times I died out of no where... After 20+ hours I am now addicted to this game. Have not seen or got sun light on my skins for DAYS!! I want my paycak on those brush campers..
Really fun once you get the hang of it however if you just run full sprint around, you will get killed. This is not for people who play games like fortnite or cod or battlefront.

Honestly just not for me.
Some may like it but eh. I haven't had any fun 4 hours in.
Too late for a refund sadly.

Honestly better then SQUAD.
There are a few mechanics Squad has that are still better, like Dragging, supplies, Map orders Building limitations,
But I can actually *SEE* enemies i'm fighting in this game VS Squad where you roam around for 45 minutes, got shot and then do it once more and the match ends. The gameplay and gunplay feel better.  it just needs a *few* things yoinked from Squad, and this can easily overtake it as a fully better game.

It is by far the most   realism  in any FPS available, Absolutely Fantastic - Also, the Lobbies are not  as toxic as some other FPS games. People want to help new players, and welcome them with open arms.

PTSD Simulator, using a mic will definitely improve you experience. People that say its a walking sim are the same people that walk through the middle of an open field, don't be like them be like Steve. Steve knows the difference between cover and concealment and how to use each to move in safety.
